missing_inputs.simple_question: Have the right outside reviewers checked the right scope?
missing_inputs.who_to_ask: Product, security, privacy/legal, AI safety, operations, trade, and payment reviewers as relevant.

missing_inputs.simple_question: Is customer data handling, security, AI use, retention, deletion, and incident handling acceptable for the launch scope?
missing_inputs.who_to_ask: Privacy/legal reviewer plus application security reviewer.

missing_inputs.simple_question: Does the product avoid unsupported customs, tariff, CFIA, sanctions, and trade-readiness claims?
missing_inputs.who_to_ask: Licensed customs broker or qualified trade/compliance reviewer.

missing_inputs.simple_question: Is there real hosted evidence for the launch scope, not only localhost?
Are live checkout, webhook, tax/refund/support, and billing wording ready, or is payment intentionally off for launch?
missing_inputs.who_to_ask: Billing/payment owner, tax/accounting advisor if needed, and support owner.

missing_inputs.simple_question: Have real target users used the product and understood what is and is not approved?
missing_inputs.who_to_ask: Product/UX owner or user research owner.

missing_inputs.simple_question: Have real buyers, suppliers, importers, exporters, or advisors validated the problem and workflow?
After all other inputs are in, is this launch approved for the exact stated scope?

next_step: Collect the missing real-person inputs, save them under external_inputs/, and rerun this script.
proof_boundary: This report changes to go-live-ready only when real dated inputs are attached. It does not approve launch by itself.
